name: Cloudflare HTTP Method Allowlist Enforcement for Sensitive Routes
objective: Enforce explicit HTTP method allowlists on sensitive application routes so only expected verbs are accepted and unexpected methods are denied.
risk_mitigated: Method tampering and verb confusion attacks against authentication/admin endpoints
Abuse of dangerous or unnecessary HTTP methods (PUT, DELETE, TRACE, CONNECT) on sensitive paths
Attack surface expansion from unneeded method exposure
description: This pack defines Cloudflare WAF custom rules that restrict HTTP methods by route class. Login/authentication endpoints are limited to GET/POST, read-only admin pages are limited to GET, and explicitly dangerous verbs are blocked on scoped sensitive paths.

rules.description: Allow only GET/POST on login and authentication routes.
Allow only GET on read-only admin/reporting routes.
Block dangerous methods on scoped sensitive routes.

deployment_notes: Deploy in log/simulate mode first (or set action to log) for at least 7 days to baseline legitimate traffic patterns.
Review matched requests by route and method before switching actions to block.
Roll out in phases: start with admin read-only routes, then login/auth endpoints, then broader sensitive path coverage.
tuning_guidance: If APIs share sensitive prefixes, carve out explicit exceptions for documented methods per endpoint.
Permit CORS preflight (OPTIONS) where required by browser clients, but scope narrowly to known API paths and origins.
For webhook or integration endpoints under sensitive prefixes, define separate allowlist rules rather than globally relaxing methods.
Keep regex path scopes aligned with application routing changes to avoid accidental over-blocking.
monitoring_notes: Track WAF events for blocked methods by URI path, source IP/ASN, and user agent to identify false positives quickly.
Alert on spikes in blocked TRACE/CONNECT/DELETE/PUT attempts on login/admin paths as potential reconnaissance or exploitation activity.
During initial enforcement, perform daily false-positive review with application owners and update route/method exceptions as needed.
